label: Transformers

node.label: Transformers
node.definition: Attention-centric residual networks process sequences with parallel content-based interaction.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Transformers is to connect Self-attention, Relative position, RoPE, ALiBi & coordinate mechanisms to downstream capabilities including Encoder–decoder transformers, Decoder-only causal transformers, Vision Transformers & Perceiver-style latent models.
examples: substrate for Vision Transformers & Perceiver-style latent models
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Self-attention, Relative position, RoPE, ALiBi & coordinate mechanisms.

dependencies.rationale: Transformers is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Self-attention.
Transformers is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Relative position, RoPE, ALiBi & coordinate mechanisms.
Transformers is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Residual, skip & highway connections.
Transformers is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Normalization & standardization.
Transformers is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Affine transforms & dense layers.

sentence_units.text: Attention-centric residual networks process sequences with parallel content-based interaction.

The role of Transformers is to connect Self-attention, Relative position, RoPE, ALiBi & coordinate mechanisms to downstream capabilities including Encoder–decoder transformers, Decoder-only causal transformers, Vision Transformers & Perceiver-style latent models.
substrate for Vision Transformers & Perceiver-style latent models

Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Self-attention, Relative position, RoPE, ALiBi & coordinate mechanisms.
